english: by 'idea and reason'. — γzuṅ-γzér (gzungs gzer) peg on a wall, = rtsig-púr (rtsig phur); a hold, support, rail, balustrade (?) Stg.

frq. spelt zuṅs (zungs), yet properly only in compounds, lit. a hold, i.e. 1. power, strength Schr.; γzuṅs-žán (gzungs zhan) Sch.: loose, weak, without a hold, untenable; γzuṅs-zád (gzungs zad) weakened, debilitated, esp. of women by loss of blood Cs.; γzuṅs-rtén (gzungs rten) prop, support. — 2. lus-zúṅs (lus zungs) the seven constituents necessary for healthy life, धातु, chyle, blood, fat, muscle, bone, marrow, semen Med. — 3. धारणी, also γzuṅs-sṅágs (gzungs sngags), spells, magic sentences, first used in the doctrine of Mahāyāna, from which the mysticism of later times originated, v. Was. (142, 177); they are for the most part but short, and always end in a string of Sanskrit syllables, that are devoid of any meaning. Whole volumes are filled with them.

wylie: gzum pa, gzur ba, gzul ba

english: Sch. 1. pannier, dosser Dzl. VV, 14. — 2. home, habitation, nest. — 3. swift, in running Thgy., quick, in comprehending Sch.
Med.; Cs.: 'a horned aquatic plant'; γzé-mai čaṅ (gze mai chang) Med. beer made of it.
for γzér-bu (gzer bu) a little nail.
looking poorly Sch.; γze-ré byéd-pa (gze re byed pa) to be poorly, ailing, ill Sch.

कण, a little grain, atom; γzeg ča čuṅ (gzeg cha chung) a small particle Lex.; γzég-ma (gzeg ma) prob. id. (Cs. also: filth?) γzeg-zán (gzeg zan) कणाद, 'atom-eater', n. of the founder of the Vaiseshika-philosophy, also called Kāsyapa; γzeg-zán-pa (gzeg zan pa) its professors Wdn.
height, loftiness, sublimity, gloriousness, esp. in γzeṅs stód-pa (gzengs stod pa), also yar γzeṅs stód-pa (yar gzengs stod pa) Pth.; to praise, extol, glorify Mil. (cf. seṅ (seng)).
I. vb. 1. v. 。dzéd-pa ('dzed pa). — 2. to hit Sch. — II. sbst. Sch.: 'a long spike'.
Cs., gen. γzéb-ma (gzeb ma), also γzebs (gzebs) Sch. 1. pannier, with lid Kun.; a box-shaped basket with lid C. — 2. cage, aviary Lex.; prison Sch. — 3. net, snare Sch.
1. Cs. = 。dzém-pa ('dzem pa). — 2. to do a thing gently, *zém-te ḍul-wa* (zem te dul wa) C. to walk softly, *žág-pa* (zhag pa) C. to put down softly.

also zer (zer), 1. nail, tack, šiṅ-γzér (shing gzer) wooden nail, lčágs-γzer (lcags gzer) iron nail; γnám-γzer (gnam gzer) 'plug or bolt for fastening a door (at the top)' Cs.; *gyáb-če* (rgyab ce) W., *gyág-pa* (rgyag pa) C., γzér-ba (gzer ba) Glr., 。dzúg-pa ('dzug pa) Lex., 。débs-pa ('debs pa) and more frq. γzér-gyis 。débs-pa (gzer gyis 'debs pa) B. to knock in, drive in, nails; lag-zér gyág-pa (lag gzer rgyag pa) driving red-hot tacks into the finger-ends, a kind of torture in C.; γzér-bu (gzer bu), vulgo *zé-ru (ze ru), zi-ru* (zi ru) a little nail. — 2. a help to memory, for retaining a lesson or doctrine, mnemonic verse Mil. — 3. ray, beam, nyi-γzér (nyi gzer) sun-beam, 。od-γzér ('od gzer) ray of light; tsa-γzér (tsa gzer) 'a hot beam', bsil-γzér (bsil gzer) 'a cool beam' (?) Cs. — 4. pain, ache, illness, (γ)zug-γzér ((g)zug gzer) id., mgo-γzér (mgo gzer) headache, rgyu-γzér (rgyu gzer) gripes, colic, p'o-γzér (pho gzer) stomach-ache Cs.; *zer-kyáṅ ṅá-la gyáb-ba rag, or táṅ-ṅa rág* (zer kyang nga la rgyab ba rag, or tang nga rag) W. I feel the pains of labour; *zer-láṅ* (zer lang) W. spasms in the stomach or something similar; γzer-。p'ríg-pa (gzer 'phrig pa) to writhe with pain; γzer 。p'ó (gzer 'pho) the pain passes from one part of the body to another S.g.

1. to bore into, drive or knock into, zer (zer) C. nails, *ná-da* (na da) C. an arrow through the ear, Chinese punishment. — 2. to feel pain, to be suffering (= γzir-ba? (gzir ba?)); čaṅ-。t'úṅ γzer (chang 'thung gzer) beer-tippling produces pain Med.

1. v. bzó-ba (bzo ba). — 2. to remember, keep in mind, own, acknowledge, esp. drin (drin) a favour, also byás-pa (byas pa), as much as to be grateful; dé-dag-gi byas-pa γzó-bai pyir (de dag gi byas pa gzo bai phyir) from gratefulness for their kindness Dzl.; byas mi γzo (byas mi gzo) they are ungrateful; drin γzó- (drin gzo-)
